mnemonics: Pandas are Series-ous about DataFrames (Series for 1D, DataFrame for 2D, Panel for 3D - though deprecated)
keyPoints: Series: One-dimensional array-like object holding data of any type with an index
DataFrame: Two-dimensional, size-mutable, tabular structure with labeled axes (rows and columns)
Index: Immutable array serving as an axis label for all Pandas objects
commonMistakes: Treating a Series like a list and using integer position instead of the index label for selection
Modifying a DataFrame without using .copy() when creating a subset, leading to SettingWithCopyWarning
